subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Map Pieces
- **19** terrain hexes (tiles)
- **6** sea frame pieces
- **9** harbor pieces
- **18** circular number tokens

#### Cards
- **95** resource cards (brick, grain, lumber, ore, wool)
- **25** development cards total
- **14** Knight cards
- **6** Progress cards
- **5** Victory Point cards
- **4** Building Costs cards
- **2** special cards (Longest Road, Largest Army)

#### Player Pieces
- **16** cities (4 of each color)
- **20** settlements (5 of each color)
- **60** roads (15 of each color)

#### Other Components
- **2** dice (1 yellow, 1 red)
- **1** robber
- **1** Game Rules & Almanac booklet

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Build frame** — Match frame piece numbers (1-1, 2-2, etc.).
2. **Place terrain** — Put all 19 terrain hexes inside the frame.
3. **Place numbers** — Add the 18 number tokens to the designated terrain hexes.
4. **Place robber** — Start the robber on the desert hex.
5. **Place special cards** — Put Longest Road and Largest Army beside the board.
6. **Prepare resources** — Sort 5 resource types into face-up supply stacks.
7. **Prepare development deck** — Shuffle development cards and place face down.

#### Beginner vs Variable Map
- **First game** — Use the beginner map from the rulebook illustration.
- **Later games** — Use variable setup from the Almanac for random islands.

#### Terrain Distribution
- **4** Forest hexes → produce Lumber
- **4** Pasture hexes → produce Wool
- **4** Fields hexes → produce Grain
- **3** Hill hexes → produce Brick
- **3** Mountain hexes → produce Ore
- **1** Desert hex → produces nothing (robber starts here)

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### All Players
- **Choose color** — Take one full color set.
- **Take pieces** — 5 settlements, 4 cities, 15 roads.
- **Take aid card** — 1 Building Costs card in your color.
- **Place start pieces** — Place 2 settlements and 2 roads during setup phase.
- **Keep reserve nearby** — Put unplaced roads/settlements/cities in front of you.

#### 3 Players
- **Beginner map note** — Do not use the red starting position shown on the map.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Beginner Setup Start
- **Preset starts** — Use the printed beginner settlement/road positions.
- **Starting resources** — Take resources from hexes touching starred starting settlements, as shown in the rules.

#### Experienced Setup Start (Snake Draft)
- **Variable board** — Build random island using Almanac setup guidance.
- **Round 1** — Starting with the oldest player, each player places 1 settlement + 1 road clockwise.
- **Round 2** — Reverse order (counterclockwise): last player from round 1 places first, starting player places last.
- **Second settlement resources** — After placing second settlement, take 1 resource card for each adjacent terrain hex.
- **Placement rules** — Each settlement must be on an unoccupied intersection with no adjacent settlements. Road must connect to that settlement.

#### First Player
- **Default rule** — Oldest player goes first.
- **Turn order** — Play continues clockwise.

#### Hidden Information
- **Resource hands** — Keep resource cards hidden from other players.
- **Development cards** — Keep unplayed development cards hidden.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Roll** — Roll both dice for resource production.
2. **Produce or 7** — Resolve normal production, or resolve robber if a 7 is rolled.
3. **Trade** — Make domestic trades and/or maritime trades.
4. **Build** — Build roads, settlements, cities, buy development cards, and optionally play a legal development card.
5. **Pass dice** — End turn and pass play clockwise.

#### Production Step
- **Settlement output** — 1 matching resource per adjacent producing hex.
- **City output** — 2 matching resources per adjacent producing hex.
- **Supply shortage** — If a produced resource cannot satisfy all eligible players, no one gets that resource for that roll.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Trading Actions
- **Domestic trade** — Active player may trade with any other player.
- **No side trades** — Non-active players cannot trade with each other.
- **Bank baseline** — Trade 4 identical resources for 1 of any type.
- **Generic harbor** — Trade at 3:1 if you have a settlement/city on a 3:1 harbor.
- **Specific harbor** — Trade at 2:1 for the pictured resource type if you have that harbor.

#### Build Actions
- **Road cost** — 1 Brick + 1 Lumber.
- **Road placement** — Must connect to your existing road/settlement/city.
- **Settlement cost** — 1 Brick + 1 Lumber + 1 Wool + 1 Grain.
- **Settlement placement** — Must connect to your road and obey Distance Rule.
- **City cost** — 3 Ore + 2 Grain.
- **City placement** — Must upgrade one of your existing settlements.
- **Development cost** — 1 Ore + 1 Wool + 1 Grain to buy top card of deck.

#### Piece Limits
- **Road cap** — Maximum 15 roads from your supply.
- **Settlement cap** — Maximum 5 settlements from your supply.
- **City cap** — Maximum 4 cities from your supply.

#### Development Card Timing
- **Play limit** — Usually play at most 1 development card on your turn.
- **New-card delay** — A card bought this turn usually cannot be played this turn.
- **VP exception** — Victory Point cards may be revealed on your turn when proving a win.
- **Progress use** — Resolve text, then remove the card from game.
- **Knight use** — Resolve robber movement/steal, then keep Knight face up in front of you.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Distance Rule
- **Spacing requirement** — You may place a settlement only if all 3 adjacent intersections are empty.
- **Applies to all colors** — Enemy and friendly settlements/cities both block placement.

#### Rolling a 7 (Robber Sequence)
1. **Stop production** — No one gains resources from dice this turn.
2. **Discard checks** — Each player with more than 7 cards discards half (rounded down).
3. **Move robber** — Active player moves robber to any other terrain hex (or desert).
4. **Steal 1 card** — Active player steals 1 random resource from one adjacent eligible opponent.
5. **Block hex** — Robber hex does not produce while robber remains there.

#### Longest Road
- **Claim condition** — First player with a continuous road of at least 5 segments takes the card.
- **Point value** — Longest Road is worth 2 victory points.
- **Transfer rule** — Card moves immediately to any player with a strictly longer road.
- **Swing impact** — Taking this card from someone creates a 4-point swing between players.

#### Largest Army
- **Claim condition** — First player with 3 played Knight cards takes the card.
- **Point value** — Largest Army is worth 2 victory points.
- **Transfer rule** — Card moves immediately to any player with more played Knights.

#### Development Card Types
- **Knight (purple)** — Move robber and steal.
- **Progress (green)** — Execute effect and discard from game.
- **Victory Point (orange)** — Keep hidden until revealed to prove win threshold.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Immediate win check** — If you have 10+ victory points during your own turn, you win immediately.
- **Off-turn 10+** — If you reach 10+ outside your turn, game continues until a player has 10+ on their own turn.

#### Victory Point Sources
- **Settlement** — 1 VP each.
- **City** — 2 VP each.
- **Longest Road card** — 2 VP.
- **Largest Army card** — 2 VP.
- **Victory Point development card** — 1 VP each (when revealed).

#### Ties
- **Practical result** — Ties are normally avoided because the game ends immediately on first valid on-turn 10+ state.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Prioritize number quality** — Favor starts touching 6/8, then 5/9, when possible.
- **Prioritize resource spread** — Your first two settlements should ideally cover at least 4 different resource types.
- **Secure expansion materials** — Ensure early Brick + Lumber access so you can actually place roads/settlements.
- **Road with purpose** — Build each road toward a specific legal settlement intersection.
- **Watch hand size early** — Spend down before opponents cycle dice and increase 7 risk.
- **Plan harbor lane** — If your production is skewed, target a matching 2:1 or generic 3:1 port path early.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Card hoarding** — Holding 8+ cards invites a crippling 7 discard.
- **Ignoring spacing** — Forgetting Distance Rule leads to dead-end roads and wasted tempo.
- **Helping the leader** — Making fair-looking trades that hand an opponent their winning build.
- **Unplanned dev buys** — Buying development cards without a follow-up resource curve.
- **Single-track scoring** — Chasing only roads or only cities while conceding Largest Army/Longest Road swings.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Expand vs upgrade** — Settlements increase coverage; cities double high-value production.
- **Road race vs Knight race** — Choose whether your 2-point swing target is Longest Road or Largest Army.
- **Trade now vs deny later** — A profitable trade this turn may still be wrong if it unlocks an opponent’s finish.
- **Port timing** — Decide whether to spend early roads on board reach or port efficiency.

metadata.notes: Primary source used: provided Catan 2015 Base Rules excerpt. Reformatted for scanability.
